---
title: "Examples of 1^st^ Law for Reacting Systems Reference Page<br/>*React Hard and React Harder* "
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
{{< include /LinkKey.qmd >}}
# Intro to the Examples of the 1^st^ Law for Reacting Systems

Applying the First Law to reacting systems requires combining **energy balances**, **heats of reaction**, and **heat capacity data** to determine the heat transferred or temperature change during a chemical reaction.

These videos work through detailed examples of energy balances for reacting systems using both the **heat-of-reaction method** and the **heat-of-formation method**.

## React Hard: The First Law Gets Chemical

This video walks through a detailed example applying the First Law of Thermodynamics to a reacting system using tabulated thermodynamic data.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/q-W5Y1d85go?si=DliOL9sV9NQYNk77" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](111-EnergeticsOfReactionsPt1Visuals.qmd)

## React Harder: Formation Heats and Alternate Endings

This video continues the example calculations and shows how the same problem can be solved using alternative thermodynamic methods.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/iqxJP6g-Qsc?si=_aE8g_VcAWvOYrmO" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](112-EnergeticsOfReactionsPt2Visuals.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

- [Material Balance Combustion Example](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/material/CombustionExample.ods) – A spreadsheet with the example calculations from *A Combustible Mixture!* The spreadsheet is reused in *React Hard* and *React Harder*.

- [Reaction Energetics Examples](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/energy/ReactionEnergeticsExample.ods) – A spreadsheet containing the example calculations in *React Hard* and *React Harder*. It demonstrates the heat-of-reaction method using two different sets of reactions and then the heat-of-formation method.

## Definitions

[Heat or Enthalpy of Formation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_enthalpy_of_formation), $\Delta \hat{H}^\circ_f$  
: The enthalpy change associated with forming one mole of a chemical species from its constituent elements at their standard temperature, pressure, and state of aggregation.  

By definition, the enthalpy of formation of an element in its standard state is zero.

[Heat or Enthalpy of Reaction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_enthalpy_of_reaction), $\Delta \hat{H}^\circ_r$  
: The enthalpy change associated with one mole of reaction for a chemical reaction as written.  

It can be calculated as the sum of the enthalpies of formation of the reactants and products multiplied by their stoichiometric coefficients, with reactant coefficients taken as negative and product coefficients as positive.

[Extent of Reaction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Extent_of_reaction), $\xi$ or $\dot{\xi}$  
: A variable describing the progress of a chemical reaction. Changes in the amount of each species are related to the extent of reaction through the stoichiometric coefficients of the reaction.

[Heat Capacity at Constant Pressure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Specific_heat_capacity#Specific_heat_capacity), $C_p$  
: The change in specific or molar enthalpy with temperature at constant pressure for a substance not undergoing a phase change.

$$
C_p \equiv \left(\frac{\partial \hat{H}}{\partial T}\right)_P
$$

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### Two videos back

**Chemical Reactions vs. The First Law: Who Wins?**  
*Forms of the 1^st^ Law for Chemical Reactions*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/mFF5hDHclPE), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/The1stLawReactingSystems.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/106-1stLawReactingVisuals.qmd)

### Previous video

**Fueling the Fire: The Shocking Truth About Combustion Reactions!**  
*Common fuels, Heats of Combustion, HHV, LHV*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/lW5dK-F6H3U), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/EnergeticsOfCombustionReactionsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/110-FuelsHeatingVisuals.qmd)

### Next video

**Resistance is Futile. Entropy Always Wins**  
*Intro to 2^nd^ Law*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/yqVUz1WA7M4), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/IntroTo2ndLawVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/113-Visuals.qmd)

### Two videos forward

**The ΔS-sentials of Calculating Entropy Changes**  
*Calculating $\Delta S$ for heat reservoirs, ideal gases, and steam*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/z2e5Rq5r288), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/CalculatingEntropyChangesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/115-CalcEntropyVisuals.qmd)
